text.title: Most Beautiful Villages of Sikkim | Lachung and Lachen | Gurudongmar Lake | Northeast India
text.u: Nestled in the Eastern Himalayas of India is a
beautiful place which is undoubtedly one of the smallest states of India.
But
some of the highest and most beautiful mountains of India reside here.
And in these mountains resides a
world where the sky is bluer than the sky.
And waterfalls flowing from the sky-touching mountains
where there are white valleys covered with snow
and fields wrapped in greenery.
Sikkim is one of the most beautiful places in India. Situated in the lap of the Himalayas, this place
has many beautiful villages, one of which
is Lachak.
Lachuk is situated in a valley surrounded by high mountains from all sides, where many snow-capped
mountains can be seen peeping from behind the mountains. And through these
mountains flows the Lajuk
[music]
river. At the same time, waterfalls flow over some mountains. The one that
is Lachuk is at almost a height of 10,000.
Such a beautiful view of the waterfall in front of you at such a high altitude.
What a beautiful waterfall one would see while sitting in the village
and that too from such close quarters, it is very beautiful,
friend. Right now
you might be able to see from here that the sun
is rising from behind this mountain and its
beautiful rays are also visible. These
are absolutely heavenly views, really
a spiritual side is visible
in this sunrise, friends.
[Music] To
explore this village further, I left the road and
started wandering through its streets. Where
I could see beautiful houses at every turn.
[Music]
Look, I found this beautiful house. There are
entire trees growing on the walls of this house. There
is so much greenery. Meaning,
starting from the bottom here, we are
covering the walls of the entire house.
This looks very beautiful, friend. It's all
full of greenery
[music].
And in this house, the one you see installed on the side, they
burn incense in it. We do
puja etc. And early in the morning
you will see this pot kept in front of every house in
which incense keeps burning.
This village has a very pure and spiritual vibe.
[Music]
Along with these beautiful houses, some small
farms or gardens are also visible. In which they
grow almost every vegetable needed.
Like here below, this local spinach is
planted from Lachum which is very
famous. And there are some apple trees
that have just started flowering. At
some places he has also cultivated potatoes etc.
Meaning there is home grown culture here.
Make it all at home. Grow it at home
and
eat it at home. And these organic vegetables grown here.
How good it is for health. I came to know about this
when I saw a grandmother of more than 70 years of age doing
farming even at this age. He
says okay, leave it, don't do all this, he
says no. So you like
farming like this? This is not a little bit, the house is
complete from the first one. You have become used to it, right? It's a
habit. It is your habit. It feels good,
meaning you should do it like this. It
feels good if you do a little work. If he doesn't work, he wo
n't be able to sit properly. No, it doesn't feel good. I don't feel like it.
Okay, great, great. Some work should be done. Should
work. Absolutely. Yes, that's right.
Pass a little time. Yes. The
name Lachung means small pass. That
is, a short route to the mountains.
Because Lachung is the only way to
go deeper into these high mountains.
[Music]
As we come up from Lachung, it
feels like the doors to a different world have opened.
Like, you see this mountain behind. It
is so beautiful. There are so many unique
mountains with so many ages. While being in the Lachos, one does
not realize that such
sights are hidden in front of one. And when we
go deep into these mountains, we come across a very
beautiful valley called Yamdhang.
[MUSIC]
[APPLAUSE]
This is the view. This is called looking so beautiful.
Meaning, all these white flags are hoisted. There are so
many mountains visible all around and behind.
Snow peaks and the river flowing right in front of me.
Such a beautiful view of the water.
What a complete view, friend. It
is very beautiful. And when I came to Lach, I was
waiting to
stand at this point of Yamdhang Valley because this
entire frame that is formed, whatever
view you see, it is absolutely picture
perfect, friend. There is a very beautiful view
here.
Seeing so many white flags here, you might be
wondering the reason for their presence here.
So the reason is that here in North Sikkim,
these
white prayer flags are put up when someone dies in the family. That too
only in a clean and very windy place
and it is believed that whenever the wind
passes through them, they spread positivity and peace.
These views become even more beautiful beyond Yamthan.
Now this mountain that you see behind me is a
very joint mountain. extremely
large. It feels like
I am standing on a movie set. It's a fantasy movie.
[MUSIC]
And it has a lot of stones down in front. This was
some old launch.
Because of that, this entire area, the entire site is lying like this. It is
filled with stones. And there's a
little bit of grass on them now. After looking at it for a while,
you will definitely feel a slight thrill after
seeing this mountain.
And while walking on this road, finally there
comes a point where this road ends
which is called zero point.
[Music]
So this is the point, and it's called the zero point,
and the road ends here.
This is the closest point you
can get to Tibet or these beautiful mountains. You
can come only up to this close and tourists are allowed beyond this.
Just a little further ahead is the road, about
1 km. And for all those army camps
and bunkers etc.
Situated at an altitude of 15,000 feet, this place remains covered with
snow for most of the year. Rather, the
water coming out from these ice glaciers
forms the Lachung River below.
All this water is melting from this glacier and going down
to Lachung. And it's
creating the entire river. And by looking at this
glacier, this blue colored
piece of ice, you will understand
how old this ice
is. The entire bluish tent is visible. It's
very old snow
[music].
And near the zero point, the
last village of this road is Yungsodon. Where the
houses of some yak herders are visible. Who
come from the lakes in search of good grass in the summer and come here to stay for a few days
[music]. The
name of the zero point is zero. But the
views here are 100 out of 100. I mean, it's such a
beautiful view. Wherever you look like this,
your gaze will stop there. It is such a
beautiful view.
[Music]
As I came back down to Lachung, I
wanted to go to this waterfall visible from Lachung. But little did I know that just by
hiking for 10 minutes from the road, I would
reach near this waterfall.
Water is falling so fast. The sound is so loud
that it can be heard far and wide.
But this waterfall that you see is not just this.
Such a big part of it from
where it is falling from the top is
not visible completely from here.
And much like
Lajuk, it was just a village in Sikkim. But on the
other side of these high mountains, there is an even more
beautiful village. But on my
way there, I saw something that was
very difficult to ignore.
So, the flood that occurred in North Sikkim two years ago has
completely destroyed this road as well, and not just this road, but the
entire mountain, houses and villages have completely
disappeared, everything has been wiped out,
only now these mountains are seen cut
by the soil. So all the mountains that you
see cut like this, brown
patches on the mountains, up to this height, the
river that is visible so far below, its water was till here
and going further it broke the Chang dam.
All the debris and all the water that flowed out created a
very dangerous scene.
All that must have been very dangerous.
And standing here now, it
feels very strange to see such a broken road.
This flood in the Teesta River was caused by the breaking of a glacier lake. Due to whose water this
village was saved by just a few meters. But
not all homes were so lucky.
[Music]
How in just a few seconds, this whole place
changed forever.
I was ready to see the beauty of nature but not this form of nature. And since then the
respect for nature has
increased even more.
But life does not stop. And now
a new path has been created again.
After some time, I reached that beautiful village
named Lachin.
[Music] Sitting
at 9000 feet, nestled in the lap of some beautiful green mountains,
Lachin is a small area filled with a number of
houses, each with
large prayer flags fluttering on its rooftops. It is
summer in the lawn right now and at
this time in the summer the temperature is -2° in the morning.
So my whole hands are freezing. The
whole feet are freezing. It
is very cold. You will
see a beautiful peak in front. It has turned golden which is
very beautiful right now, i.e. at the time of sunrise.
This snow-covered mountain, visible in every view of Lachen,
is Lamo Akam, whose height is about
20,000 feet, which makes these views of Lachen
even more beautiful.
[Music]
So right now I'm walking around the streets of Nachin
and one thing I noticed here is
that there are all kinds of houses here. Right now, as
you can see behind me, this is a wooden house.
What you see behind him is the Mud
House. The bottom is completely made of mud and the top is made of
wood. And there are pucca houses here.
Now the number of pucca houses
is increasing. But it is amazing. Otherwise I
see either wooden houses or mud houses.
I find the wooden houses the most beautiful among all these houses.
I
also feel like building a wooden house.
Wooden houses look very beautiful.
How beautiful it is. And behind there is such a beautiful
backdrop of mountains. While
roaming around like this, I also got a chance to see a house from inside.
Which was a house made of mud. Can I see from inside
hello.
[Music]
Hello.
How old is this house? Now that has happened, it has been
80 years. It's been 80 years.
Okay, okay, so this is good, a mud
house is good? It gets hot. It gets hot.
Well, it is very warm in the cold months.
Yes, its roof is made of
wood. Yes, there is a sheet on top. Okay okay.
This is lucky. All those walls
are pot. The walls are made of mud. This is made of clay.
Yes.
Yes, it is all soil. This is all soil.
Why keep the kitchen open like this? It
is like this. This has been happening traditionally since before. It happens
traditionally. It is slightly opened in the second house. After
innovating, you did
not innovate here.
Just opposite to today's era of modular kitchens, there is a
tradition of displaying all the kitchen utensils in the house.
After some time uncle asked, will you have tea
and then
take this one from here, oh ho
thank you, what do you call it, kabe
khabaje, ok, ok, it is like a
salty ...
Saltpare. Salt and mercury. Yes. Have you guys also had
tea? Did you drink it? I drank it well. After having
tea and local snacks here, I left.
Visit Lachin. Thank you so much. I liked it
very much.
[Music] The
beautiful Lachan Monastery is built at the highest point here.
[Music]
Very vibrantly colored building and
definitely the most peaceful
place in Lachen. But the most beautiful thing about this monastery is
its lamp house.
So many lamps are burning together.
Seeing this, it makes him feel so good, it is
very positive. And it is so hot in this room.
I'm feeling hot right now, not wearing a jacket.
And this entire jacket became hot.
I am heated up. They are
kept closed like this so that these lamps do not go off. Let the
wind not accidentally come in from inside and extinguish these lamps. That is
why doors and windows always
remain closed. There's just a chimney up there
[MUSIC].
Like the name Lachum means small pass. The
name Lachen means
Big Pass. Because Lachen gives way to the
highest mountains of Sikkim
and the beauty of the first place on this route is
astonishing.
[Music]
This place, covered in snow at an altitude of 13,000 feet, is
Thangoveli. The
crystal clear Teesta River flowing through the snow-capped mountains and
some snow-covered wooden houses situated along the river. These
scenes once and for all erase the difference between reality and dream.
I just came a little ahead of Lachin
and here midway I found out that everything had turned
white because it had snowed the whole night. It
looks so beautiful.
Everything is completely white and there is a
layer of white everywhere. Like there are trees
, wires, prayer flags, there
is a layer everywhere. You look very beautiful, friend.
But these mountains of snow were just the beginning. Of
higher and more beautiful
views to come. But before going there, I
want to tell you one special thing. Nowadays
AI has come everywhere and is making everything easier.
So imagine what it would be like if
AI makes the task of making videos easier.
Well, there is no need to think. Now this has become a
reality. Yes, indeed,
with Artlist's AI Image & Video feature, you can now turn
any of your video ideas
into reality. That too
in just a few minutes. Let me tell you how. First of all,
go to this AI feature of Artlist and
tell us the idea of ​​your video.
Enter text and then enhance that text
for detailing. And then AI will generate a high
quality image of your idea.
And then you can convert that image into a video by clicking on Animate.
And you can generate
all the shots for your videos in just a few minutes.
And the best part is that you can
also generate voice overs for the story or narration of those videos on Artlist itself.
Rather, music, sound
effects and if needed, footage also. You get
it all in one place
with a single subscription
with Artlist Max. If you want, you
can try it by taking a one month subscription
and if you like it then only take the annual subscription in
which
you will also get two months extra free from the link in my description. Then it is
worth trying.
Beyond Thangu Valley, as the height
increases beyond 13,000 feet, the views also change.
In just a few minutes, those
greenery and snow-covered mountains
appear to transform into a cold desert.
So, from Lachin to here, the
altitude has increased a lot and the
temperature has decreased a lot. Right now no
trees etc. are visible here.
Where trees were still visible. There are no trees here now. The
area has become completely like a cold desert. It feels like
I am standing in Ladakh. I am not even standing in Sikkim.
But it is really very
beautiful. Just
look at this mountain behind me. Both the mountains and the clouds are mixed. I do
n't know the full extent yet.
It is white, it is the white of snow or the
white of clouds and it looks very beautiful, friend. It's
very beautiful
[music].
While travelling on these routes, the altitude
increases up to 18,000 feet. And after reaching this height,
within a short time,
Gurudon, one of the tallest lakes of India, appears in front.
[Music] Nestled
right in the middle of the Eastern Himalayas, this beautiful
blue water body is nestled in Gurudambaram. A
strong breeze
creates small waves in the water. Apart from their voices,
everything is quiet here.
So quiet that even the silence can be heard.
Rather, it is so quiet that you can hear every breath you take.
But not just because it's peaceful here.
Also because it is at a
height of 17800 feet. This is such a high
altitude that the oxygen here is
only half as compared to sea level.
So welcome all of you to India.
Gurudo Lake is one of the highest lakes in India and this is an unbelievable view.
Which I can't believe. Such
beautiful water and that mountain in front,
friend, there is no mountain more beautiful than this, it is a very
beautiful mountain. I am standing at a height of about 17800 feet and
I am still out of breath. It is becoming
very difficult.
I can't say much. But this lake
is really beautiful, man.
[Music]
Gurudambar Lake
is barely 5 km from the Tibet border. And because of its proximity to the border,
only
Indian tourists can come here. But
this place is very sacred for the people living here.
Because there is a part of this writer that does
n't freeze no matter how cold it gets. And
people here believe that that part of the writer was
touched by the Buddhist guru Padma Sambhava.
And for this reason this article is called Guru Domber
article.
Such a peaceful place surrounded by nature
slowly changes you. Where
we are not used to so much peace or silence in cities. One feels
a little uneasy as soon as one comes to such places.
But then after some time that peace starts
giving us comfort. Nor is
life as slow as
we are used to here. Because
in this era of 10-minute delivery in cities, here
some things still have to be brought on foot for hours.
But this anxious wait
also gradually turns into patience.
Because such places change you slowly.
And as you move at this slow pace,
you
gradually start considering yourself a part of these places.
hello.
But this change hidden within us also
teaches us that there is not always a need to change the place for this.
Just need to change the speed.
[Music]
[Music]
